name: dict.get with default
general_feedback: dict.get(key, default) returns d[key] if the key is present and default otherwise, without raising. Here "b" is missing, so 0 is returned.
correct_feedback: Correct — Key 'b' is missing, so the provided default is returned.

answers.feedback: Key 'b' is missing, so the provided default is returned.
get returns None only when no default is supplied; here the default is 0.
dict.get never raises KeyError — it returns the default instead.
1 is d['a']; get was called with key 'b', not 'a'.
